# excel-kit

> Fluent API-based Excel/CSV read-write Java library for Java 17+

excel-kit is a streaming Excel/CSV library built on Apache POI (SXSSF). Columns are defined programmatically via builder chains or lambda configs — no annotations.

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/dornol/excel-kit
- Javadoc: https://dornol.github.io/excel-kit/
- Maven Central: io.github.dornol:excel-kit

## Quick Reference

- Write Excel (typed): `ExcelWriter.<T>builder().build().column("Name", T::getName).column("Age", T::getAge, c -> c.type(ExcelDataType.INTEGER)).write(stream).toFile(path)`
- Write Excel (multi-sheet): `ExcelWorkbook.builder().build().sheet("Sheet1").column(...).write(stream)` then `wb.finish().toFile(path)`
- Write Excel (map): `ExcelWriter.forMap("Name", "Age").write(stream).toFile(path)`
- Read Excel (setter): `ExcelReader.setter(T::new).column("Name", T::setName).required().build(in).read(r -> ...)`
- Read Excel (mapping): `ExcelReader.mapping(row -> new Record(row.get("Name").asString())).build(in).read(r -> ...)`
- Read Excel (map): `ExcelReader.forMap().build(in).read(r -> r.data().get("Name"))`
- Write CSV: `new CsvWriter<T>().column("Name", T::getName).write(stream).toFile(path)`
- Read CSV: `CsvReader.setter(T::new).column("Name", T::setName).build(in).read(r -> ...)`

## Core Classes

- ExcelWriter<T> — single-type Excel writer with lambda config and auto-rollover
- ExcelWorkbook — multi-sheet workbook with different data types per sheet
- ExcelSheetWriter<T> — per-sheet writer within ExcelWorkbook (lambda config style)
- ExcelWriter.forMap(...) — write Map<String, Object> without typed POJOs (static factory on ExcelWriter)
- ExcelTemplateWriter — write into existing Excel template files
- ExcelReader<T> — streaming Excel reader (SAX-based)
- ExcelReader.forMap() — read Excel into Map<String, String> (static factory on ExcelReader)
- CsvWriter<T> — streaming CSV writer (CsvWriter.forMap(...) for map-based writing)
- CsvReader<T> — CSV reader (OpenCSV-based)
- CsvReader.forMap() — map-based CSV reading (static factory on CsvReader)
- ExcelKitSchema<T> — unified schema for both reading and writing
- ExcelHandler / CsvHandler — consume-once output wrappers

## Column Configuration

All column config methods (available via lambda configurer on ExcelWriter, ExcelSheetWriter, CsvWriter):

Data: type(ExcelDataType), format(String), dropdown(String...), validation(ExcelValidation)
Layout: width(int), minWidth(int), maxWidth(int), alignment(HorizontalAlignment), verticalAlignment(VerticalAlignment), wrapText(), rotation(int), indentation(int)
Font: bold(boolean), fontSize(int), fontName(String), fontColor(ExcelColor), strikethrough(), underline()
Background: backgroundColor(ExcelColor), cellColor(CellColorFunction)
Border: border(ExcelBorderStyle), borderTop/Bottom/Left/Right(ExcelBorderStyle)
Header: headerFontColor(ExcelColor) — per-column header font color override
Structure: group(String), outline(int), hidden(), locked(boolean), comment(Function)
Null: nullValue(Object) — default value for null cells (inheritable via defaultStyle)

## Data Types

STRING, INTEGER, LONG, DOUBLE, DOUBLE_PERCENT, DATE, DATETIME, FORMULA, HYPERLINK, RICH_TEXT, IMAGE, BOOLEAN

## Writer-Level Options

headerFontName(String), headerFontSize(int), defaultStyle(Consumer), autoFilter(), freezePane(int), freezePane(int cols, int rows), rowHeight(float), rowColor(Function), maxRows(int), tabColor(ExcelColor), protectSheet(String), protectWorkbook(String), password(String), summary(Consumer), chart(Consumer), conditionalFormatting(Consumer), printSetup(Consumer), beforeHeader(BeforeHeaderWriter), afterData(AfterDataWriter), onProgress(int, ProgressCallback)

## Reading Features

Column mapping: name-based, index-based (columnAt), positional, required() for mandatory fields
Read modes: setter (mutable), mapping (records/immutable), map (no POJO)
CellData: asString(), asInt(), asLong(), asDouble(), asBigDecimal(), asLocalDate(), asLocalDateTime(), as(Function), isEmpty()
RowData: get(String), get(int), has(String), size(), headerNames()
Config: sheetIndex(int), headerRowIndex(int), password(String), onProgress(int, callback)
Methods: read(Consumer), readStrict(Consumer), readAsStream()
Note: FileHandler.write() throws unchecked exceptions (ExcelWriteException, CsvWriteException), not IOException
Discovery: ExcelReader.getSheetNames(in), ExcelReader.getSheetHeaders(in, sheet, header)

## Optional: Full Documentation

For detailed examples and API usage, see META-INF/AI.md inside the JAR artifact, which links to topic-specific documentation files.
